_doc: Material compatibility graph — cross-category rules within a (style, room) cell. Rewriter applies these so that objects from different categories visually belong to the same set.

rules.rule: Wooden parts of lighting fixtures (lamp bases, shelf-mounted lights, pendant ring) use the same wood as furniture wood_primary in this cell.
Lighting fixture finish (lamp arms, switch plates, pendant cord clamps) must match metal_primary defined in cell palette.
Any textile element (curtain swatch, rug, cushion, throw, towel) must use fabric_main as primary, fabric_accent as secondary.
Stone surfaces (countertop, vanity, side table top, coffee table top) all use the cell's stone material.
Promt may include exactly ONE accent color from accent_color. No other saturated colors. Background and frame always pure white.
All finishes obey finish_note. japandi/scandi → matte/honed only. modern_classic → polished and gleaming.
Kitchen island top, peninsula top, and main counter top all use the same stone material from the palette.
Cabinet handles, drawer pulls, and tap body use the same metal finish (metal_primary).
All bathroom fixtures (tap, shower head, towel rail, robe hook, mirror frame, shower glass profile) use metal_primary. metal_accent only for one small detail.
Coffee table top and side table tops use wood_primary, same as living-room floor planks.
Headboard upholstery and curtains use fabric_main; bed throw uses fabric_accent.
Pendant lamps are sized proportionally to the room: kitchen → larger over island; bedroom → small bedside; bathroom → low ceiling slim flush; living → statement pendant.
main = primary materials (wood_primary, fabric_main, etc.). alt = secondary materials (wood_secondary, fabric_accent). Both stay within palette.
If alt2 variant exists, it differs from main/alt only by FORM (silhouette, dimension, configuration), not by material. Same palette materials.
Every prompt ends with 'isolated on pure white background, soft studio lighting, ambient occlusion, centered front view, professional product photography, 8k'. No exceptions.
Materials = swatch / sample size (~30×30cm). Furniture = full-object front view. Lighting = full-fixture eye-level. Decor = scale-of-hand for small items.
Never include people, hands, or visible body parts. Pure product photography only.
No text, brand names, signs, or logos anywhere in the image.
Embrace wabi-sabi — visible grain, natural pores in stone, subtle plaster texture. Avoid perfect symmetry.
Strong symmetry, classical proportions (golden ratio), beveled and chamfered edges, ornate detailing where appropriate.
Slim profiles, lifted-off-floor furniture (visible legs), open weave, plenty of negative space.
